title: Bardiel
wikitext:
[]: Bardiel             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Oct 13, 2007 11:23 am hm...i've always wondered, what does Bardiel look like? is he just that slimy goo stuff you see try to penetrate Unit-00s arm? i kno he's a parasite, but even parasites have bodies

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Oct 13, 2007 12:07 pm There's also that webby stuff inside the entry plug when they pull Toji out, the display had it labeled as the 13th Angel.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sat Oct 13, 2007 2:09 pm Bardiel is probably just goo. (I swear to god, if someone tries to fanwank this....)

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Sat Oct 13, 2007 2:20 pm well heres what I heard I heard that bardiel is a goo It is very simmillar to the goo the romans used to build thier ships the roman ships signfy corruption which in turn relates back to bardiel corrupting unit three. Im actually serious.....psych!

[]:             Quote Post by Fiore » Sat Oct 13, 2007 2:29 pm Maybe I'm crazy...But wasn't Bardiel was part of/inside chillin'/masquerading as that cloud Unit 03's flown through just before it lands at Matsushiro? Which to me makes sense because in that episode, which I happened to watch last night, the pilot of the craft mentions something about the cloud before flying through it... And as the plane and Eva pass through you see lightning/electricity surge about inside the cloud for a second or two. So maybe it looks like a cute, fluffy, cloud 'till it manifests itself in Unit 03 and gets all pissy?

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sat Oct 13, 2007 2:41 pm FANWANK TIME Bardiel is green goo. Dollar bills are green. Bardiel is therefore symbolic of the evils of capitalism. At the end of every episode, the announcer says "Jikai Yokoku". The announcer's voice is obviously female. An anagram of "jikai" is "kaiji". Remove the "i" and you get "Kaji". Therefore, the "Jikai Yokoku" announcer is a post-sex change Kaji. Therefore, it becomes obvious that Kaji faked his death and took drastic measures to conceal his identity. Asuka pilots an Eva. Hitler's wife was Eva Braun. Asuka is German. Therefore, Asuka is a Nazi.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Sat Oct 13, 2007 2:46 pm chee wrote: FANWANK TIME Bardiel is green goo. Dollar bills are green. Bardiel is therefore symbolic of the evils of capitalism. At the end of every episode, the announcer says "Jikai Yokoku". The announcer's voice is obviously female. An anagram of "jikai" is "kaiji". Remove the "i" and you get "Kaji". Therefore, the "Jikai Yokoku" announcer is a post-sex change Kaji. Therefore, it becomes obvious that Kaji faked his death and took drastic measures to conceal his identity. Asuka pilots an Eva. Hitler's wife was Eva Braun. Asuka is German. Therefore, Asuka is a Nazi.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sat Oct 13, 2007 2:53 pm That's not all. See the color of her plug suit and Unit 02? She's a MARXIST Nazi.

[]:             Quote Post by Fiore » Sat Oct 13, 2007 3:01 pm chee wrote: Asuka pilots an Eva. Hitler's wife was Eva Braun. Asuka is German. Therefore, Asuka is a Nazi.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Oct 13, 2007 3:44 pm TOPIC, BITCHES

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sat Oct 13, 2007 8:33 pm Ain't much we can do, Tines. Bardiel is likely the sticky goo that stops the entry plug from ejecting, and the same stuff that seeps on Eva-00's arm. However, I don't think he's in the entry plug. It says "Entry Plug-13th Angel", which I take to mean "Entry Plug-EVA 03", because Eva-03 had been reclassified as the 13th Angel following the hijack.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sat Oct 13, 2007 11:33 pm I always thought that Bardiel was a sentient atomized fog-substance that could solidify at will and change its physical state (solid, liquid, gas, plasma?) and retain its intellect, much like Iruel, all members acting as one unit, much like cells to human beings. Probably (if what i believe is true) it is much stronger intellectually and otherwise when it's a liquid or solid state. But the size of the singular unit of Bardiel (and maybe Iruel?) are most likely extremely extremely small, maybe smaller than the nano-scale, which would explain how it got inside the entry plug. Remember that Ritsuko did not investigate how it got inside the plug, just to "salvage" it, which we still do not clearly know what that means...anyways, I feel like i'm beginning to not mayke anymore sense...so i'll stop now.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 14, 2007 12:24 am Bardiel proper isn't even "good"; it's a bacteria sized cloud of infection; sort of like Iruel. But it takes over Eva 03.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Oct 14, 2007 7:26 am UrsusArctos wrote: Bardiel is likely the sticky goo that stops the entry plug from ejecting, and the same stuff that seeps on Eva-00's arm. However, I don't think he's in the entry plug.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Oct 14, 2007 7:33 am Tch! Anno makes Americans look pretty stupid...why else would the Bardiel goo get in the entry plug with nobody noticing? Yeah, I saw that...I thought it was LCL at first... Bardiel goo, all over. ICK!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Oct 14, 2007 7:47 am If you think that's bad: the entry plug BLEEDS after Sho crushes it.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 14, 2007 12:34 pm There's sticky stuff IN the entry plug. Look more closely. And, yes, the sticky/gooey stuff is "the real Bardiel".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Oct 14, 2007 12:44 pm Perhaps a good question to pose would be why no one noticed anything until that moment of the startup experiment. Did Bardiel really undergo some kind of instantaneous shift from being stealthily microscopic to gooily macroscopic? So the USA, and even Ritsuko, weren't "dumb for not noticing goo in the entry plug"

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Oct 14, 2007 12:55 pm Perhaps a good question to pose would be why no one noticed anything until that moment of the startup experiment. Did Bardiel really undergo some kind of instantaneous shift from being stealthily microscopic to gooily macroscopic?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Oct 14, 2007 8:00 pm V wrote: I mean, Iruel wasn't very detectable until it just started spreading like crazy (they thought it was rust)
